data.title: Fitch Maintains Novo Banco's 'BBB' IDR on Rating Watch Positive
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Paris-28 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has maintained Novo Banco S.A.'s 'BBB' Long-Term Issuer Default Rating (IDR) on Rating Watch Positive (RWP). Fitch has also affirmed the 'bbb' Viability Rating (VR). A full list of rating actions is below.

The RWP reflects our view that if the bank is acquired by Groupe BPCE (GBPCE; A/Stable) the IDRs would very likely be based on shareholder support from a higher-rated owner. This reflects the likely strategic importance of the Portuguese operations to the new parent, providing geographical diversification. Upon completion of the acquisition, Fitch will likely assign a Shareholder Support Rating, withdraw the Government Support Rating (GSR) and resolve the RWP. RWP resolution may take longer than six months, as the transaction is likely to close in 1H26, subject to regulatory approvals.

Fitch has revised the Portuguese banks' operating environment to 'a-'/Stable' from 'bbb+'/Stable. The Portuguese banking sector has significantly increased its resilience to shocks due to loan book deleveraging and structurally improved asset quality, profitability and capitalisation. We expect lower private indebtedness together with falling interest rates and above-eurozone-average GDP growth to support business opportunities for Portuguese banks. The bank's only moderate franchise compared with larger competitors in Portugal limits the immediate positive impact from the stronger operating environment.

Key Rating Drivers

Business and Risk Profiles Drive Ratings: Novo Banco's ratings reflect its adequate franchise in Portugal and stable business model geared towards corporate and retail banking, tightened risk profile and stable, deposit-rich funding profile. Novo Banco's healthy profitability is a rating strength as it benefits from significantly reduced credit risks, resulting in good internal capital generation. We do not expect the planned acquisition to materially affect the bank's strategy and business model.

Adequate Franchise: Novo Banco is the fourth-largest bank in Portugal with market shares of about 9% and 10% in deposits and loans, respectively. The successful restructuring transformed Novo Banco into a cost-efficient and simplified bank.

Improved Risk Profile: Novo Banco's portfolio has a slightly lower share of residential mortgages than Portuguese peers. Underwriting standards are prudent and in line with industry standards. The bank has reduced its earnings sensitivity to lower interest rates.

Better Asset Quality: Novo Banco's impaired loans ratio has decreased since end-2023 and was 3.2% at end-September 2025, close to Portuguese and other Southern European peers. The ratio should reduce below 3% in 2026-2025, underpinned by resilient domestic GDP growth, further portfolio cleaning and conservative loan underwriting. High impaired loans coverage at 96% mitigates risks from unexpected credit losses. The legacy foreclosed real estate assets decreased to a manageable 0.6% of total assets at end-September 2025.

Strong Profitability: Novo Banco's profitability compares well with most mid-sized southern European peers. It benefits from a good domestic franchise, sound cost efficiency and high net interest margins, like domestic peers. Novo Banco's 9M25 operating profit remained high and reached 4.5% of risk-weighted assets (RWAs; 2024: 3.6%), benefiting from lower credit losses. We expect this ratio to remain strong at above 3.5% in 2026-2027, despite lower interest rates. Revenue will be supported by further growth of fees and pick-up of loan growth.

Satisfactory Capital Buffers: Novo Banco's buffers over regulatory requirements have improved significantly since end-2022, due to materially stronger profitability resulting in high capital generation. The fully loaded common equity Tier 1 (CET1) ratio was 16.9% at end-September 2025. We expect it to remain above 16% in 2025-2026, excluding potential significant share buy-backs or extraordinary dividends after the acquisition by GBPCE.

Stable Funding, Adequate Liquidity Buffer: The funding is mainly sourced from a stable and granular retail deposit base. The loans/deposits ratio was about 88% at end-September 2025, according to Fitch's calculation. Novo Banco's successful issuance of senior debt and covered bonds illustrates strengthened investor confidence. The bank uses unsecured wholesale funding mainly for compliance with minimum requirements for own funds and eligible liabilities (MREL).

Rating Sensitivities Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

We would likely remove Novo Banco's IDRs, senior preferred and deposit ratings from RWP if it is not acquired by GBPCE, although this is unlikely.

Negative rating pressure on the VR could arise from an increased risk appetite and asset-quality deterioration leading to operating profit/RWAs below 1.5%, the impaired loans ratio above 4% and CET1 ratio falling materially below 12% for an extended period.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

Once Novo Banco is acquired by GBPCE, its Long and Short-Term IDRs will likely be support driven and would likely be upgraded to 'A-' and 'F1', respectively. We expect Novo Banco's Long-Term IDR to initially be one notch below that of GBPCE, due to low immediate post-transaction synergies with the new parent, the likely lengthy integration period for funding and capital management, and GBPCE's limited record of operations in Portugal.

An upgrade of Novo Banco's VR would require a stronger business profile, underpinned by growing market shares and lower reliance on net interest income, further asset-quality improvements, and the maintenance of satisfactory buffers over capital requirements. This may be reflected, for example, in an impaired loans ratio closer to 2%, operating profit/RWAs consistently above 3.5%, and the CET1 ratio remaining at least about 15%.

OTHER DEBT AND ISSUER RATINGS: KEY RATING DRIVERS

The RWPs on Novo Banco's Short-Term IDR, senior preferred and deposit ratings mirror that on the Long-Term IDR.

The Short-Term IDR of 'F3' is the lower of two options mapping to a Long-Term IDR of 'BBB', driven by Novo Banco's funding and liquidity score of 'bbb'.

The long-term deposit rating is one notch above Novo Banco's Long-Term IDR, reflecting full depositor preference in Portugal and the structure of the bank's resolution buffer, which includes senior preferred debt. The short-term deposit rating of 'F2' is the lower of two options mapping to a long-term deposit rating of 'BBB+', driven by Novo Banco's funding and liquidity score of 'bbb'.

The long-term senior preferred debt rating is aligned with the bank's IDRs because we expect it will meet its MREL with a combination of senior preferred and junior instruments. We do not expect the buffer of senior non-preferred and more junior debt to exceed 10% of RWAs. The short-term debt rating is aligned with the Short-Term IDR.

GSR

Novo Banco's 'no support' GSR reflects our view that although external extraordinary sovereign support is possible it cannot be relied upon. Senior creditors can no longer expect to receive full extraordinary support from the government in the event that the bank becomes non-viable. The EU's Bank Recovery and Resolution Directive and the Single Resolution Mechanism for eurozone banks provide a framework for resolving banks that requires senior creditors participating in losses, if necessary, instead of or ahead of a bank receiving sovereign support.

OTHER DEBT AND ISSUER RATINGS: RATING SENSITIVITIES

Novo Banco's senior preferred, and deposit ratings are primarily sensitive to changes in its IDRs. The short-term IDR, senior preferred and deposit ratings are also sensitive to the funding and liquidity score. In addition, the senior preferred long-term debt rating could be upgraded if Fitch expects that Novo Banco will either meet its MREL without recourse to senior preferred debt or if the buffer of senior non-preferred and more junior debt exceeds 10% of the resolution group's RWAs on a sustained basis.

An upgrade of the GSR would be contingent on a positive change in the sovereign's propensity to support the bank. In Fitch's view, this is highly unlikely, although not impossible.

VR ADJUSTMENTS

The asset quality score of 'bbb' is above the 'bb' implied category score due to the following adjustment reason: historical and future metrics (positive)

The earnings and profitability score of 'bbb+' is below the 'a' implied category score, due to the following adjustment reason: revenue diversification (negative)

The capitalisation and leverage score of 'bbb+' is below the 'a' implied category score, due to the following adjustment reason: risk profile and business model (negative)

The funding and liquidity score of 'bbb' is below the 'a' implied category score due to the following adjustment reason: deposit structure (negative)

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. Public Ratings with Credit Linkage to other ratings

The RWP on Novo Banco's IDRs is linked to potential support from GBPCE.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visithttps://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: Sterling Remains Resilient After U.K. Budget — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling has performed relatively well after this week's U.K. budget, despite increased expectations of the Bank of England cutting interest rates in December, Ebury's Matthew Ryan says in a note. The absence of major surprises in the budget announcements has contributed to market calm and supported sterling, Ryan says. A higher fiscal headroom of around 22 billion pounds has also contributed to market stability, he says. "The fiscal headroom is important in that the greater buffer should limit the likelihood that Labour will need to hike taxes again this time next year." Sterling falls 0.2% against a broadly stronger dollar to $1.3214 but rises slightly to 0.8751 per euro. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Russia's Gazprom posts Q3 net income of $1.72 billion after a year-ago loss
data.description:
Russia's energy giant Gazprom posted on Friday a net income of 134.2 billion roubles ($1.72 billion) for the third quarter after a net loss of 53 billion roubles a year ago, when it was hit by an one-off tax, the company said on Friday.

Revenue for the quarter reached 2.18 trillion roubles, down from 2.4 trillion roubles a year ago.

($1 = 78.1500 roubles)
